classObjective: Apply the Law of Detachment and the Law of Syllogism to draw valid conclusions from conditional statements.
workedExamples: 12-3, Example 2 — Determine Whether a Conclusion Is Valid Using the Law of Detachment
12-3, Example 3 — Apply the Law of Syllogism to Draw Conclusions
warmUp: If a number is even, it is divisible by 2. 8 is even. What can you conclude?
conceptDevelopment: Mini-lesson: Law of Detachment (affirming hypothesis); Law of Syllogism (chaining conditionals); valid vs. invalid conclusions; common logical errors
guidedPractice: Teacher-led practice applying Laws of Detachment and Syllogism to draw valid conclusions
independentPractice: Practice problems determining validity of conclusions and applying logical laws
assessment: Exit ticket: use the Law of Syllogism to draw a conclusion from: If it rains, the game is cancelled. If the game is cancelled, practice is indoors.
capReflection: How does the Law of Syllogism extend the Law of Detachment to chains of conditional statements?
